title: All of them or none
checkLabels.t7: You didn't use style on the tag
why.empty: The editor is empty. Hit the bin and start again.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: You write in styles.css. index.html isn't touched.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed.
hint2.not-closed: If you've edited index.html, hit the bin.
why.unlinked: The page can't find styles.css. You've edited index.html.
hint2.unlinked: Hit the bin and work only in styles.css.
why.no-h1: There is no heading. You've edited index.html.
why.no-p: There are no paragraphs. You've edited index.html.
hint2.h1-not-red: A rule for h1 with the color property. The value is darkred.
why.p-not-brown: A paragraph is left without colour — or not all of them are brown. The check looks at all three.
hint2.p-not-brown: One rule for p catches every paragraph at once. Don't write three.
hint3.p-not-brown: If you tried to colour just one of them — that's exactly the lesson. By tag you can't. Go back and colour all three.
hint4.p-not-brown: p { color: saddlebrown; } — one rule, three paragraphs.
why.no-line-height: The line height isn't 24 pixels. The lines are stuck to each other.
hint2.no-line-height: The property is line-height. It goes in the same p rule.
hint3.no-line-height: line-height isn't the gap BETWEEN lines. It's the height of the line itself — the text sits in the middle of it. That's why 24px with a 16px font gives 8 pixels of air, split above and below.
hint4.no-line-height: Add line-height: 24px; to the p rule.
why.used-inline: You used style on the tag. I understand why — you wanted to reach just one paragraph. There's no way, not with what you know. Remove it.
hint2.used-inline: That's the whole point of this lesson: a tag selector can't tell one paragraph from another. The tool comes next time.
hint3.used-inline: The attribute works, but it's a step backwards — it put you back where you were in lesson 01. Three elements, three copies of the same thing.
hint4.used-inline: Delete the style attribute. Make all three paragraphs the same and accept that the third one can't be different yet.
why.lost-p: A paragraph is gone. The work is in styles.css.
blocks.0.text: You've known the tag selector since lesson 02. p catches the paragraphs, h1 catches the heading. Now you'll see how far it goes.
blocks.2.text: The heading dark red. All three paragraphs brown, with a line height of 24 pixels.
blocks.3.text: Then, before you submit, do one more thing: try to make ONLY the last paragraph — the one about Christmas — a different colour. Seriously, try.
blocks.5.text: You write p and catch all of them. You write p again — you catch all of them again, only now you've overwritten the first rule. There's no way to say "the third one", because a tag selector doesn't know there is a third. It knows what a thing is, not which one it is.
blocks.6.text: The only way out, with what you have, is to go back to the style attribute on the tag. It works. And it puts you exactly where you were in lesson 01 — three elements, three repetitions, nothing learned.
blocks.7.text: That's why this check rejects it. Not because it's forbidden, but because it isn't the answer.
blocks.9.text: What you're missing is a way to PUT A LABEL on one element and then call it by that label. HTML has such a thing — you used it in the HTML course without needing it. CSS is what it was waiting for.
blocks.10.text: The next lesson is about it.
